methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $1M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Axial Composites is a Seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Giuseppe Rapisarda that has raised $1M+. The valuation assessment draws from a broader Robotics & Manufacturing sector peer universe of 9 private companies and 1 public company (277810.KQ), including comparables such as 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics. However, no same-stage or adjacent-stage peers with sufficient data density exist to support peer-median triangulation.

The Stage-Progression method is the applicable valuation technique, using the company's $1M total raised as a proxy for current value and applying a 2.5x multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage progression. This yields an implied valuation of $2.5M. Peer-Median analysis was not applied due to insufficient comparable datapoints: while 11 same-sector peers exist, none met the narrow filters for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-tier classification—a minimum of three datapoints is required to avoid misleading conclusions.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $2.1M (low) to $2.9M (high), with a median estimate of $2.5M. This range reflects reliance on a single methodology and carries material uncertainty given the absence of stage-matched peer benchmarks. Key uncertainties include validation of the 2.5x step-up assumption against this specific company's traction and differentiation, and lack of near-peer public market signals specific to Seed-stage robotics ventures.
